m00_the_signal.title: AMOC FovS Enters Third Consecutive Anomaly Week
m00_the_signal.body: The RAPID array has recorded a third consecutive week of anomalous freshwater flux at the FovS monitoring line, consistent with accelerated Greenland meltwater injection into the North Atlantic. Three independent modelling groups — Potsdam Institute, NCAR, and CSIRO — have revised AMOC collapse probability upward. Current ensemble range: 34–62% before 2100, versus 10–25% in IPCC AR6. Three-week persistence elevates this from an episodic signal to a sustained early-warning indicator.

items.headline: AMOC FovS — Third consecutive anomaly week
items.why_it_matters: Sustained signal elevates from episodic to early-warning indicator. Ensemble collapse probability revised to 34–62% before 2100.

items.headline: Thwaites grounding line retreats 1.4 km — exceeds IPCC upper projection
items.why_it_matters: Once initiated, marine ice cliff instability is self-sustaining. 3.3 metres SLR equivalent at stake.

items.headline: Amazon vegetation stress: 450,000 km² below baseline — approaching dieback threshold
items.why_it_matters: At ~19% deforestation, approaching lower bound of the carbon sink → net emitter transition threshold.

items.headline: Sahel: 27 million in IPC Phase 3+, Lake Chad aquifer depleting beyond seasonal variance
items.why_it_matters: Full F1 cascade active: water stress → food insecurity → displacement → political instability.

items.headline: EU CSRD Phase 2 in force — 50,000 additional companies, mandatory climate risk disclosure
items.why_it_matters: First binding double materiality requirements for EU financial sector. Enforcement baseline being established.

boundaries.headline: Atmospheric CO₂ concentration at 424 ppm; global mean surface temperature +1.45°C above pre-industrial baseline
boundaries.summary: Current trajectory is consistent with 2.7°C by 2100 under current NDC commitments. No evidence of non-linear departure from trend this week.

boundaries.headline: E/MSY rate 10–100× above background; BII below 80% threshold across key biomes
boundaries.summary: Amazon vegetation stress anomaly (450,000 km²) and Lake Chad basin degradation are active contributors this week.

boundaries.headline: Global forest cover loss continues; Amazon at ~19% deforestation of pre-industrial extent
boundaries.summary: Southern and eastern Amazon degradation is the primary driver of this boundary's continued transgression in Q1 2026.

boundaries.headline: Green water (soil moisture) boundary transgressed; GRACE-FO confirms aquifer depletion in Lake Chad Basin
boundaries.summary: Sahel groundwater depletion beyond seasonal variance constitutes the primary freshwater signal this week.

boundaries.headline: Nitrogen and phosphorus cycles remain ~2–3× above safe operating boundary
boundaries.summary: No new material development this week. Boundary remains transgressed at persistent baseline levels.

boundaries.headline: 140,000+ synthetic chemicals released; <3,000 tested for toxicity — governance void persists
boundaries.summary: Attribution gap: no binding international governance mechanism for novel chemical releases. ISA framework for deep-sea mining remains unenforced.

boundaries.headline: Mean ocean surface pH at 8.05, down from pre-industrial 8.18; saturation states declining in polar regions
boundaries.summary: Aragonite saturation thresholds being crossed in Arctic and Southern Ocean surface waters — direct threat to calcifying organisms underpinning marine food webs.

boundaries.headline: IMO 2020 sulphur cap reducing aerosol masking effect — potential warming acceleration
boundaries.summary: Aerosol reduction from cleaner shipping fuels is removing a masking cooling effect. This represents a paradox: a public health improvement accelerating near-term climate forcing. No new primary-source quantification this week.

boundaries.headline: Ozone layer recovery on track; Antarctic ozone hole within normal seasonal parameters
boundaries.summary: Montreal Protocol continues to deliver. No material development this week.

tipping_systems.proxy_metric: FovS freshwater flux at 34°S — three consecutive anomaly weeks
tipping_systems.headline: FovS sustained anomaly — strongest early-warning signal since metric established
tipping_systems.summary: Third consecutive week of anomalous freshwater flux. Collapse probability ensemble: 34–62% before 2100 (IPCC AR6 baseline: 10–25%). Cross-monitor flag: EGHTM.

tipping_systems.proxy_metric: Grounding line retreat rate: 1.4 km/year — exceeds IPCC AR6 upper bound
tipping_systems.headline: Grounding line retreat exceeds IPCC upper projection — marine ice cliff instability dynamics observed
tipping_systems.summary: Self-sustaining once initiated. 3.3 m SLR equivalent. NASA IceSat-2 January 2026 data.

tipping_systems.proxy_metric: Vegetation greenness anomaly: 14% below 2001–2020 baseline across 450,000 km²
tipping_systems.headline: Amazon approaching lower bound of dieback threshold — 19% deforestation of pre-industrial extent
tipping_systems.summary: INPE and Global Forest Watch cross-validation. Southern/eastern Amazon concentrated. At 25–30% threshold, system may cross from carbon sink to net emitter.

tipping_systems.headline: Greenland meltwater contributing to North Atlantic freshwater anomaly and AMOC signal
tipping_systems.summary: Greenland mass loss is the primary driver of the FovS anomaly. No new primary-source data this week beyond the AMOC signal.

tipping_systems.proxy_metric: Siberian thermokarst expansion continuing; atmospheric CH₄ at 1923 ppb
tipping_systems.headline: Permafrost carbon pool estimates revised upward — 1,700 Gt C vs prior 1,500 Gt estimate
tipping_systems.summary: No step-change event this week. Baseline trajectory worsening. Self-reinforcing feedback loop not yet confirmed but precursor conditions strengthening.

tipping_systems.proxy_metric: Global bleaching event in progress — fourth mass bleaching event since 1998
tipping_systems.headline: Fourth global mass coral bleaching event confirmed — NOAA Coral Reef Watch alert level 2
tipping_systems.summary: Over 50% of the world's coral reefs are currently experiencing bleaching-level heat stress. Attribution gap: no binding international liability framework for bleaching losses. Structural F4 case.

m03_threat_multiplier.cascade_chain: Freshwater depletion → food insecurity → displacement → political instability
m03_threat_multiplier.f1_trigger: Lake Chad Basin aquifer depletion beyond seasonal variance
m03_threat_multiplier.tier_1_physical: GRACE-FO satellite groundwater data confirms accelerating aquifer depletion in Lake Chad Basin
m03_threat_multiplier.tier_2_human: 27 million in IPC Phase 3 (Crisis) or above across G5 Sahel states (FEWS NET Q1 2026)
m03_threat_multiplier.tier_3_political: Displacement is the primary driver of irregular migration into Libya and coastal West Africa, exploited by Russian FIMI operations. Junta governance correlation flagged to Democracy Monitor.
m03_threat_multiplier.reverse_cascade_check: Armed conflict in Sudan and Mali is actively preventing adaptation investment and destroying agricultural infrastructure — confirmed reverse cascade.
m03_threat_multiplier.headline: Sahel water-food-displacement cascade confirmed active — reverse cascade from conflict also identified

m03_threat_multiplier.cascade_chain: Glacial retreat → seasonal water stress → agricultural productivity loss → political instability
m03_threat_multiplier.f1_trigger: Indus Basin glacial melt acceleration — Karakoram and Hindu Kush
m03_threat_multiplier.tier_1_physical: Himalayan glacier mass loss rate at 65 cm water equivalent/year (2023 WGMS data) — 2× the 2000–2009 average
m03_threat_multiplier.tier_2_human: Pakistan's 2022 flood event reduced agricultural GDP by 3.8%. Seasonal water stress is structurally increasing for 200 million dependent on glacial-fed rivers.
m03_threat_multiplier.tier_3_political: Agricultural stress is a persistent destabilisation factor in Pakistan–India bilateral relations and a driver of domestic political instability in Pakistan.
m03_threat_multiplier.reverse_cascade_check: India–Pakistan nuclear posture and conventional military competition increases wildfire and disruption risk to Himalayan ecosystems — reverse cascade assessed as moderate and ongoing.
m03_threat_multiplier.headline: Indus Basin glacial retreat is a multi-decade structural cascade driver — South Asian political instability is a second-order outcome

m03_threat_multiplier.cascade_chain: Sea ice loss → new maritime access → resource competition → governance vacuum → geopolitical escalation
m03_threat_multiplier.f1_trigger: Arctic sea ice extent at record low for March 2026 — Northern Sea Route navigable for 180+ days/year
m03_threat_multiplier.tier_1_physical: Arctic sea ice minimum area declined 13% per decade since 1979. Northern Sea Route transit season has extended from 30 to 180+ days.
m03_threat_multiplier.tier_2_human: New fisheries, shipping, and hydrocarbon access zones are creating competing jurisdictional claims between Russia, Norway, Canada, and the US.
m03_threat_multiplier.tier_3_political: Russian Arctic military buildup and hybrid operations using newly accessible sea routes constitute a confirmed cross-monitor signal to EGHTM.
m03_threat_multiplier.reverse_cascade_check: Russian energy extraction from newly accessible Arctic zones (oil/gas) directly accelerates climate forcing — confirmed reverse cascade to Earth system transgression.
m03_threat_multiplier.headline: Arctic sea ice loss is operationalising new geopolitical competition — confirmed reverse cascade from Russian resource extraction

m04_extreme_weather.polycrisis_filter: 2024–2025 long rains failure follows three consecutive failed seasons in parts of Ethiopia, Somalia, and Kenya. The 90-day cascade pathway: continued failure → IPC Phase 4 (Emergency) declaration → mass displacement activating existing political fault lines in Somalia (al-Shabaab territorial operations) and Ethiopia (Tigray reconstruction delays).
m04_extreme_weather.systemic_attribution: ENSO teleconnection confirmed; underlying baseline drying trend from ITCZ northward shift — itself linked to AMOC weakening via atmospheric coupling. F1 applied: event crosses into political basin within modelled timeframe.
m04_extreme_weather.headline: East African failed rains — 90-day cascade pathway leads to IPC Phase 4 and political destabilisation in Somalia

icj_tracker.last_development: Oral hearings concluded December 2024. Written submissions phase ongoing. Advisory opinion expected 2025–2026. No new development this week.

loss_damage_tracker.disbursed: USD 0 (no disbursements confirmed as of Q1 2026)
loss_damage_tracker.gap: USD 661 million pledged, $0 disbursed — 100% disbursement gap in first compliance cycle

items.headline: EU CSRD Phase 2 in force — mandatory climate risk disclosure for 50,000+ companies including financial sector
items.summary: Double materiality requirements now binding for EU financial institutions. First reporting obligations due 2027. Phase 1 enforcement track record is the compliance baseline to monitor.

m06_ai_climate.headline: IEA projects data centre electricity demand to double by 2028 — direct competition with decarbonisation targets
m06_ai_climate.summary: Data centre electricity demand is growing at 15–20% per year globally. In high-grid-carbon economies (India, Southeast Asia, parts of the US), AI compute scaling is directly competing with net-zero pathway electricity budgets. Water consumption for cooling is an additional constraint in water-stressed regions.

m06_ai_climate.headline: Google DeepMind GenCast outperforms ECMWF ensemble for 15-day forecasts — AI weather modelling operationalised
m06_ai_climate.summary: GenCast (December 2024 Nature paper) achieves better skill scores than the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts ensemble on 97% of forecasting targets. The system enables ensemble-quality forecasting at 50× computational cost reduction. Operational deployment for extreme weather early warning is underway.

m07_biosphere.headline: Ocean heat content reaches new record — 2025 NCEI data confirms sustained anomaly
m07_biosphere.summary: The top 2000m of the global ocean absorbed more heat in 2025 than in any previous measured year. Ocean heat drives intensification of Atlantic and Pacific hurricane seasons and accelerates coral bleaching and marine ecosystem disruption.

m07_biosphere.headline: Fourth global coral bleaching event confirmed — NOAA Alert Level 2
m07_biosphere.index: NOAA Coral Reef Watch Bleaching Alert
m07_biosphere.summary: Over 50% of coral reefs globally experiencing bleaching-level heat stress. Structural F4 case: no binding international liability framework for bleaching losses. This is the fourth event since 1998; intervals between events are shortening.

m07_biosphere.headline: Deep-sea mining — ISA exploitation regulations still absent; new applications pending
m07_biosphere.summary: The International Seabed Authority has failed to finalise exploitation regulations despite a 2023 deadline. Multiple mining applications are in review. Deep-sea polymetallic nodule extraction would destroy benthic ecosystems that have never been commercially fished or monitored at baseline. F4 applied: governance void persists.

m08_geostrategic_resources.headline: China controls 60–80% of critical mineral processing for EV and renewable energy supply chains — export control risk escalating
m08_geostrategic_resources.summary: China's dominance in cobalt (80% of global refining), lithium carbonate processing (70%), and rare earth separation (90%) constitutes a structural supply chain chokepoint for the energy transition. US and EU critical minerals strategies are attempting to build alternative supply chains but face 10–15 year lead times. Cross-monitor flag: EGHTM.

m08_geostrategic_resources.headline: GERD fourth filling cycle complete — Egypt's strategic red line on Nile flow remains unresolved
m08_geostrategic_resources.summary: The Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam fourth filling reduced downstream Nile flow to Egypt below Egypt's declared minimum threshold. Egypt has repeatedly stated this constitutes a strategic red line. No binding treaty framework governs GERD operations. Sudan is caught between upstream and downstream interests. F2 applied: governance mechanism absent.

m08_geostrategic_resources.headline: Northern Sea Route transit season at 180+ days — Russian Arctic governance posture hardening
m08_geostrategic_resources.summary: Sea ice loss has transformed the Northern Sea Route from a seasonal curiosity into a strategic maritime corridor. Russia is asserting unilateral jurisdiction over transit rules, in conflict with UNCLOS freedom of navigation. Arctic governance vacuum is a direct product of Earth system change creating new strategic terrain. Cross-monitor flag: EGHTM.

flags.linkage: Arctic sea-ice loss is operationalising new Russian hybrid operations via newly accessible Northern Sea Route. Critical mineral supply chain chokepoints (China processing dominance) are a direct energy transition vulnerability for EU strategic autonomy. Sahel ecosystem collapse generating displacement vectors exploited by Russian FIMI operations in West Africa and France.
flags.gerp_perspective: From Environmental Risks: Earth system change (Arctic sea-ice loss, Sahel drying) is directly generating new geopolitical operating terrain and hybrid threat vectors. These are not analogies — they are causal pathways.

version_history.reason: Established on publication of first GERP brief

flags.linkage: Environmental stress is a structural driver of democratic backsliding. 14 of the 20 most food-insecure states score below the autocracy threshold on the V-Dem liberal democracy index. Sahel cascade is a confirmed F1 pathway from physical water stress to political instability. AMOC weakening producing Sub-Saharan monsoon disruption is an unmodelled mechanism that could accelerate this correlation beyond current projections.
flags.gerp_perspective: From Environmental Risks: V-Dem index movements in high-exposure states (Sahel, Horn of Africa, South Asia) are a leading indicator that BII/MSA degradation is translating into political instability. This is the F1 cascade in its final form.

version_history.reason: Established on publication of first GERP brief

flags.linkage: Climate disinformation and greenwashing narratives are documented FIMI vectors deployed by state actors to delay climate policy and fracture multilateral consensus. Where state attribution is confirmed in the FIMI Monitor, this constitutes a reverse F1 cascade — a geopolitical operation accelerating an Earth system transgression. Sahel narrative operations and migration policy manipulation in European states are a material intersection.
flags.gerp_perspective: From Environmental Risks: any confirmed state attribution for climate denial campaigns in the FIMI Monitor constitutes a reverse cascade — a geopolitical intervention accelerating planetary boundary transgression. This should be flagged at CRITICAL severity.

version_history.reason: Established on publication of first GERP brief

flags.linkage: Data centre electricity demand doubling by 2028 (IEA projection) directly competes with net-zero pathway electricity budgets. AI compute scaling drives critical mineral demand (rare earths, cobalt) — direct linkage to M08 Geostrategic Resources. Positive edge: AI weather modelling (GenCast) is enabling cost-effective extreme weather early warning at scale.
flags.gerp_perspective: From Environmental Risks: AI is simultaneously a planetary boundary risk (energy/water/minerals consumption) and a boundary monitoring enabler (precision forecasting, deforestation detection). Escalation trigger: when compute scaling crosses a quantifiable environmental threshold, this flag moves to CRITICAL.

version_history.reason: Established on publication of first GERP brief
